summary: Weld a cold-open onto the FRONT of a finished master with the branded film-burn transition (frame-jerk + warm burn overlay + two-click sound) at the junction, then encode.

parity.note: The weld FILTERGRAPH — the film-burn overlay, the old-film frame-jerk offset expression and the two-click audio delay — is a pure function of params, pinned byte-identical in tests/test_op_edit_weld.py, so the transition the viewer sees is exact. The terminal ENCODE defaults to h264_nvenc (not byte-reproducible across GPU/driver) and degrades to libx264 on a box without NVENC, so bit_exact would be a lie; perceptual is the honest mode for anything whose final encode is NVENC. The flare detection is a deterministic probe of the burn clip, not a decision.

cold_trim.description: Seconds trimmed off the cold-open END before the weld — a creative dial the brain authors, defaulted to 0. Not a threshold.

gain.description: Film-burn click level (0..1 typical), defaulted to 0.4. A brand/creative dial, not encoder tuning.
